title: Cloud LTX: warm-on-connect (hide the ~3:30 first-gen cold tax)
description: Re-scoped 2026-06-28: the node-58 removal half is DISPROVEN+dropped (see below); only warm-on-connect remains. WARM-ON-CONNECT (app-side, no rebuild): after a Pod connects + ComfyUI is ready, fire a tiny throwaway LTX gen in the background and INTERRUPT it the moment the stage-1 sampler emits its first step (we already get that via the comfy:step SSE). That faults the 40GB transformer into VRAM invisibly (paying the one-time cold tax in the background) so the user's FIRST real gen is already warm instead of ~3:30-5:00 cold. User framing: 'let it reach the first sampler and stop, then it's warm.' Likely wire into the connect/ready path in routes/remoteProxy.js + comfyController; reuse a minimal LTX workflow at lowest res/1-frame; discard output. NOTE: even after warm-up, each gen still re-faults per stage (~40s transition) - warm-up only removes the ONE-TIME cold start, does not make gens 36s (that 'warm-resident' claim was a mislabel - see pod-perf-investigation.md). DROPPED: ~~remove MpiClearVram node 58~~ = DISPROVEN LIVE 2026-06-28 (5090 Pod): bypassing it made a warm single-shot 119s vs ~90s WITH it; aimdo re-stages the 40GB regardless, the node's unload_all_models() is load-bearing for SPEED (clean stage-2 fault slate). Node 58 kept, edit reverted. Full context: docs/builder/research/pod-perf-investigation.md top banner. BRANCH RunPod, app-side only.
